text: Our instructors are competent and knowledgeable about the Bible and the selected lessons each week.
I am fully confident the answers given during sessions are not based on opinion or personal perspective.
The Sunday School sessions are well-organized and structured in a way that facilitates learning.
The instructors are always able to guide and keep the class on-topic
The instructor makes learning the Bible exciting.
Occasionally, I fall asleep, loose focus, or start to think about unrelated Sunday School activities during class sessions.
I feel that my participation and contributions are valued during Sunday School sessions.
I often feel pressured to share my input and perspective during class sessions.
We spend too much time focusing on politics, religious denominations, and/or other controversial topics during our Sunday School lessons.
I love to study God’s Word (The Bible) and believe that Sunday School is an essential component of Christian lifestyle.
I am interested in continuing my Sunday School education as a teacher.
I feel that the Sunday School sessions contribute positively to my understanding and application of biblical teachings.
Sunday school is a waste of time. I can learn better studying the Bible at home.
After each class, I can easily summarize what I have learned in 2 sentences or less.
I anticipate the opportunity to get up and make it to Sunday school on time (9 am) so that I do not miss.
I’ve learned so much about the Bible during my commitment to attending Sunday School each week.
Sunday School is an excellent way to break down and  understand the Bible in small consumable increments.
Our instructors introduce creative ways to encourage full class participation, engagement, and collective learning activities.
I can confidently connect and apply the Sunday school Bible lessons to my life.
I am a faithful member of Bethesda Temple Church of Alton.
I often listen to my instructors and feel like I could do a better job teaching the lesson.
I take the time to study my lesson before class to be better prepared to share.
Comments
